_comment: Each entry controls one case-file card on case-files.html. status: 'active' | 'retired'. published: true means the writeup is linked and readable; false means a locked/redacted teaser card is shown instead. To publish a writeup: set published to true, commit, push. That's it.

machines.title: Hercules
machines.summary: Full domain compromise chain from an unauthenticated web foothold through certificate services abuse to a Domain Controller.

machines.title: Cobblestone
machines.summary: SQL injection escalated from a read primitive to arbitrary file write, used to gain remote code execution.

machines.title: PingPong
machines.summary: Cross-forest trust abuse chaining a certificate-based privilege grant into delegated access on a downstream SQL host, then back to domain admin via template abuse.

machines.title: Garfield
machines.summary: ACL misconfiguration chained through logon-script code execution into an RODC compromise, forged Golden Ticket, and a KeyList attack against the primary DC.

machines.title: Pirate
machines.summary: Legacy machine-account password recovery leveraged into gMSA credential theft, NTLM relay to RBCD, and a WriteSPN-based path to the Domain Controller.

machines.title: Fries
machines.summary: Linux-to-Windows pivot across a hybrid environment via database RCE and tunneled access to certificate services.

machines.title: Nimbus
machines.summary: Blocklist bypass on an SSRF filter led to cloud credential theft and an escape from a privileged build container.

machines.title: Checkpoint
machines.summary: Recovered a deleted AD object to pivot into a delegated Managed Service Account takeover, confirmed via VM memory analysis.

machines.title: DevHub
machines.summary: Unauthenticated RCE on an MCP debugging tool, pivoted through a Jupyter terminal WebSocket into an internal API that leaked a root SSH key.

machines.title: Abducted
machines.summary: Unauthenticated command injection in a printer share's print command, chained through a leaked backup config and a Samba share misconfiguration to root.

machines.title: SmartHire
machines.summary: Insecure pickle deserialization in an ML model registry led to RCE, escalated to root via a sudo-permitted tool's plugin-loading mechanism.

machines.title: Enigma
machines.summary: A world-readable NFS share leaked credentials that chained through two webmail accounts into an admin-support RCE and a root-owned internal service.

machines.title: CCTV
machines.summary: Default credentials on a video-management platform led to blind SQL injection, hash cracking, and a client-side-only validation bypass to root.

machines.title: Kobold
machines.summary: Unauthenticated RCE in an MCP debugging tool, lateral movement through a world-writable directory, and a Docker management API used for a host filesystem escape.

machines.title: Silentium
machines.summary: A password-reset token leak led to unsandboxed server-side code evaluation, a leaked container credential, and a symlink-based arbitrary file write to root.

machines.title: Connected
machines.summary: Unauthenticated SQL injection in a PBX admin panel abused database event privileges for RCE, escalated via a signed automation hook's pipe injection flaw.

machines.title: Reactor
machines.summary: A framework-level RCE exposed database credentials, and a root-owned Node.js debug inspector left open on localhost provided the path to full compromise.

machines.title: Cap
machines.summary: An IDOR on a scan-history endpoint leaked another user's packet capture, exposing plaintext FTP credentials reused for SSH; a stray cap_setuid capability on Python gave root.

machines.title: MakeSense
machines.summary: A publicly-shipped client-side "encryption" key enabled a blind stored XSS against an automated admin reviewer, escalated to RCE and an internal OCR service's file-write primitive.

machines.title: Helix
machines.summary: An anonymous-write NiFi instance gave RCE via a JDBC driver abuse, and an exposed industrial OPC UA protocol was used to force open a privileged maintenance window.

machines.title: Logging
machines.summary: A credential leaked in an SMB-hosted sync log chained through Shadow Credentials and a scheduled-task DLL hijack into a rogue-WSUS ADCS abuse for SYSTEM.

machines.title: Polluted Logs
machines.summary: A Node.js prototype pollution challenge built from the ground up, including the vulnerable service and intended exploit path.

machines.title: Grid Down
machines.summary: Path traversal / RCE challenge modeled on CVE-2021-41773, built as an infrastructure-themed target for competition play.
